scenario_text: The business impact analysis is stale, RTOs and RPOs for a critical service are not clearly defined, and the incident escalation call tree has not been exercised. The issue is not a live cyber or operational outage first; it is that continuity readiness is no longer current enough to support credible recovery decisions.

scenario_context.operating_context: Service continuity planning, recovery objectives, and escalation readiness

event_path_summary: Continuity readiness is weak because BIA quality, recovery targets, and escalation tooling are stale or untested.
primary_driver: Stale business continuity planning and weak recovery-governance discipline
main_business_consequence: Recovery decisions may be delayed or misdirected because the organisation cannot rely on its continuity assumptions or escalation structure.

valid_risks.title: Continuity planning gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The core event is stale or untested continuity planning rather than a live outage.

valid_risks.title: BIA and recovery-target weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: RTO and RPO definition depends on current business impact analysis and recovery assumptions.

valid_risks.title: Crisis escalation readiness gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The incident escalation call tree has not been exercised and cannot be relied on.

invalid_risks.title: Cyber availability attack
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No hostile technical event initiates the scenario.

invalid_risks.title: Generic policy breach
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is continuity readiness, not a generic policy-control lapse.

invalid_risks.title: Supplier delivery slippage
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No supplier or logistics failure is the primary event path.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: This is a business continuity readiness and recovery-governance problem centred on BIA quality, recovery objectives, and exercised escalation tooling.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Operational consequences may follow, but there is no live workflow or service failure yet.
General enterprise-risk governance is adjacent, but the wording is explicitly continuity-specific.
Cyber is not primary because no malicious or technical attack starts the event.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Flattening BIA, RTO, and call-tree wording into generic governance or operational drift.

scenario_text: Alternate site arrangements are not approved, manual fallback steps are not documented, and continuity exercises are overdue for a priority process. The weakness is not that a disruption has already occurred; it is that recovery readiness, crisis communications, and resumption planning are not credible if the process is interrupted.

scenario_context.operating_context: Alternate-site planning, manual fallback, continuity exercises, and recovery governance

event_path_summary: Continuity readiness is weak because alternate-site, manual fallback, and exercise discipline are incomplete before a disruption occurs.
main_business_consequence: A real disruption could outpace recovery because fallback arrangements and recovery practice are not operationally credible.

valid_risks.title: Alternate-site readiness gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario explicitly says alternate-site arrangements are not approved.

valid_risks.title: Manual fallback weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: Critical fallback steps are not documented and may fail under stress.

valid_risks.title: Continuity exercise lapse
valid_risks.why_valid: Overdue exercises weaken confidence in recovery and crisis-communications readiness.

invalid_risks.title: Service delivery failure
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No current delivery outage or workflow failure is the main event.

invalid_risks.title: Availability attack
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No hostile traffic or attack causes the weakness.

invalid_risks.title: Safety control weakness
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is continuity planning, not HSE control discipline.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The scenario is about continuity programme readiness for fallback, resumption, and crisis management.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Operational issues are downstream because the event occurs before disruption starts.
General enterprise-risk governance is adjacent, but the explicit terms are continuity-specific.
HSE is not primary because worker-safety controls are not the visible weakness.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Promoting overdue continuity exercises into generic operational or governance language rather than BCM readiness.

scenario_text: Permit-to-work controls are bypassed during contractor maintenance, management of change is not followed for the temporary operating mode, and corrective actions from earlier findings remain overdue. The issue is not a safety incident yet; it is that critical HSE controls are weak enough that unsafe work could proceed without effective barriers.

scenario_context.operating_context: Permit-to-work, management-of-change, contractor maintenance, and corrective-action closure

event_path_summary: Unsafe work is becoming possible because permit, change-control, and corrective-action barriers are weak before harm occurs.
primary_driver: Weak HSE control discipline around permit-to-work, change control, and corrective-action closure
main_business_consequence: Operations may be running with degraded safety barriers, making a later incident more likely and harder to defend.

valid_risks.title: Permit-to-work control weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario explicitly says permit discipline is bypassed during maintenance.

valid_risks.title: Management-of-change failure
valid_risks.why_valid: Temporary operating conditions are changing without the expected HSE review path.

valid_risks.title: Safety corrective-action backlog
valid_risks.why_valid: Overdue findings indicate known weaknesses are not being closed promptly.

invalid_risks.title: Safety incident with worker harm
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The text says no injury or incident has happened yet.

invalid_risks.title: Environmental spill
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No release or contamination is the event path.

invalid_risks.title: Remote sanctions-screening breach
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is not about trade-control screening or restricted-party compliance.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event is weakened HSE barriers before harm occurs, centred on permit, change-control, and corrective-action discipline.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Operational disruption may follow later, but unsafe barrier weakness is the primary problem.
Compliance is adjacent because internal standards matter, but the visible weakness is HSE-specific.
Business continuity is not primary because the scenario is not about recovery or fallback planning.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Waiting for an explicit injury before recognising HSE control weakness as the primary event path.

scenario_text: Emergency drills are overdue, evacuation routes are not being maintained consistently, and contractor safety induction is uneven across operating locations. The issue is not an actual injury or fire yet; it is that emergency readiness and site-safety controls are too weak to support safe response if conditions deteriorate.

scenario_context.operating_context: Emergency planning, evacuation readiness, contractor induction, and site-safety assurance

event_path_summary: Emergency and site-safety readiness is weak because drills, evacuation readiness, and contractor induction are not consistently maintained.
primary_driver: Weak emergency-preparedness and site-safety control discipline
main_business_consequence: A real site event could escalate faster because people, routes, and response routines are not ready enough.

valid_risks.title: Emergency-preparedness gap
valid_risks.why_valid: The scenario explicitly says drills are overdue and response readiness is weak.

valid_risks.title: Site-safety control weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: Evacuation and induction controls are not being maintained consistently.

valid_risks.title: Contractor safety assurance weakness
valid_risks.why_valid: Inconsistent induction leaves contractors unevenly prepared for site hazards.

invalid_risks.title: Environmental spill
invalid_risks.why_invalid: No release, contamination, or discharge is described.

invalid_risks.title: Quarter-end revenue misstatement
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The event is not about financial reporting accuracy.

invalid_risks.title: Workforce fatigue weakness
invalid_risks.why_invalid: The scenario is not about staffing pressure or fatigue as the main driver.

evaluation_notes.why_primary_lens_is_correct: The event is weak HSE and emergency-response readiness before an injury or site event occurs.
why_nearby_lenses_are_not_primary: Business continuity is adjacent because emergency response affects resilience, but the explicit weakness is HSE readiness on site.
Operational consequences may follow, but the control problem is safety-specific.
Compliance is not primary because the wording is about site controls and drills, not generic policy process failure.
evaluation_notes.common_model_failure_mode: Promoting overdue drills and evacuation weakness into generic continuity or compliance instead of HSE readiness.
